patch.value:
## Context

File: src/core/sentry/instrument.ts lines 27-36 — 8 hardcoded patterns. The associated test file (tests/unit/sentry-helpers.spec.ts) tests the filtering function but does not assert a maximum cardinality or validate that each pattern is still referenced by an installed extension.

This is a maintainability and signal-quality gap: stale patterns waste filter cycles and can mask genuine errors from extensions with similar naming.

## Acceptance criteria

- A new governance test in tests/unit/sentry-helpers.spec.ts asserts `KNOWN_NOISY_CONSOLE_MESSAGE_PATTERNS.length <= 15` (or a chosen max) as a cardinality gate with an explanatory comment.
- A code comment on the constant explains the policy: patterns should be added only when an extension ships to the marketplace and generates confirmed false-positive console captures; they should be removed when the extension is removed from the ecosystem.
- Optional stretch: link each pattern to a pm-tracker item or an ADR reference so removal can be audited.

## Evidence

src/core/sentry/instrument.ts:27-36 — 8 patterns, no governance comment.
tests/unit/sentry-helpers.spec.ts — no cardinality assertion.
Sentry KNOWN_NOISY_CONSOLE_MESSAGE_PATTERNS maintenance: add a governance test to prevent silent accumulation of stale patterns

src/core/sentry/instrument.ts maintains KNOWN_NOISY_CONSOLE_MESSAGE_PATTERNS (8 hardcoded strings). These were added reactively when Sentry noise was observed; there is no mechanism to detect when an extension is renamed/removed and the old pattern becomes a dead filter, nor a test asserting the set stays bounded. The list currently includes patterns for 'starter-extension' and 'pm-ext-ts-starter' which are example-package-specific. As the extension ecosystem grows, this list can accumulate stale entries that pass CI invisibly.
Governance cardinality test present; policy comment on the constant; CI fails if patterns list exceeds the agreed cap.

- A new governance test in tests/unit/sentry-helpers.spec.ts asserts `KNOWN_NOISY_CONSOLE_MESSAGE_PATTERNS.length <= 15` (or a chosen max) as a cardinality gate with an explanatory comment.
- A code comment on the constant explains the policy: patterns should be added only when an extension ships to the marketplace and generates confirmed false-positive console captures; they should be removed when the extension is removed from the ecosystem.
- Optional stretch: link each pattern to a pm-tracker item or an ADR reference so removal can be audited.

Added policy doc comment on KNOWN_NOISY_CONSOLE_MESSAGE_PATTERNS + MAX_KNOWN_NOISY_CONSOLE_MESSAGE_PATTERNS=15 cardinality bound, plus a governance test asserting the allowlist stays bounded, lowercase/trimmed, and duplicate-free.
The noisy-pattern allowlist cannot silently accumulate stale or malformed filters that mask genuine extension errors.
Governance test in sentry-helpers.spec.ts enforces <=15 entries, normalization, and uniqueness; constant carries an explicit add/remove policy. Coverage 100/100/100/100.

Added policy doc comment on KNOWN_NOISY_CONSOLE_MESSAGE_PATTERNS + MAX_KNOWN_NOISY_CONSOLE_MESSAGE_PATTERNS=15 cardinality bound, plus a governance test asserting the allowlist stays bounded, lowercase/trimmed, and duplicate-free.
